steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank follows the noun "reactions" and precedes the prepositional phrase "within molecules." A word in this post-nominal position must be a participle, not a finite verb.

steps.title: Apply the non-finite modifier rule and eliminate
steps.content:
Only a present or past participle can modify a noun in post-nominal position. The four choices are:

A. occurred — past tense finite verb; cannot modify a noun. Eliminated.
B. occurring — present participle; correctly modifies "reactions" as a non-finite adjective ("reactions occurring within molecules"). Correct.
C. occur — base-form finite verb; cannot modify a noun. Eliminated.
D. will occur — future-tense finite verb; cannot modify a noun. Eliminated.

B is correct.
